mentions.note: Limited redemptions on flagship private credit fund treated as non-event by market; stock up 7.5%, best S&P performer; fear narrative around private credit redemptions fading.
Up $7 today (from ~$4 crisis lows); private equity credit crisis appears limited or 'cornered off'; short squeeze reflects contained damage risk.

CEO Jack Dorsey firing 4,000/10,000 (40%) using AI as rationale; represents thesis that AI enables smaller teams; Cramer worries this is failure of imagination vs. Jensen Huang's 'do more with more' approach.
Preferred over Blue Owl in private equity/credit space; 5% yield, better-run management, higher quality.

Jack Dorsey cut workforce 40% (10k→6k) for AI tools; stock rallied low-50s to high-60s. Model may become industry standard; if Big Tech adopts, stocks benefit from efficiency gains and capital deployment.
Assets under management surged to $14 trillion; reported phenomenal earnings; investment banks finally getting their due on strong capital markets activity.
Most money under management of any company on earth; emphasizing new fund types; should report pretty good number but may face sector competition pressure.
